palette: Volcanic charcoal and oxidized copper, with highlights of arctic blue and sulfur yellow.
lighting: Low-angle arctic afternoon light, long blue shadows, a slight atmospheric haze from geothermal steam.
instagram_prompts: First-person POV (FPP) macro focus on a textured, porous black basalt rock surface. A single drop of mineralized water is caught mid-fall, reflecting a nearby geothermal plant's steam plume in its curve. In the blurred background, the jagged snow-capped peak of Mount Esja rises under a pale indigo sky. Low-angle arctic light. No human/nanobot in frame. High-fidelity cinematic photography.

FPP view from a high-altitude balcony in the Holt neighborhood, Reykjavik. Below, the colorful rooftops of the city are dusted with fresh snow. In the distance, the sleek, geometric fans of the Mammoth direct air capture plant are visible on the horizon, silhouetted against a fading orange sunset. Long blue shadows stretch across the streets. No human/nanobot in frame. Sharp, atmospheric architectural photography.

FPP extreme close-up on the weathered, reclaimed wood of a desk at the Eyja Guldsmeden Hotel. A small, glass vial of basalt dust sits beside a handwritten notebook. Through the window, the soft glow of the Imagine Peace Tower's blue light beam begins to cut through the twilight sky over the harbor. Lighting is a mix of warm indoor lamplight and cool outdoor dusk. No human/nanobot in frame. Moody, detail-oriented still life.
